chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P. No. D-3318 of 2021 [Allah Nawaz Memon v. Government of Sindh & others] Date Order with signature of Judge(s) Before: Mr. Justice Adnan-ul-Karim Memon Mr. Justice Muhammad Hassan Akbar Date of hearing and Order: 25.03.2026 Mr. Jazib Aftab advocate for the petitioner. Syed Samiullah Shah, advocate for respondent-LERP. Mr. Abdul Jalil Zubedi, AAG. ********** O R D E R Adnan-ul-Karim Memon, J. – Petitioner has filed this Constitution Petition under Article 199 of the Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan 1973, seeking the following relief(s):- “i. Direct the Respondent No. 3 to pay the pending & outstanding salaries of the Petitioner amounting to a total of Rs. 4,498,545/ аррroximately, forthwith. ii. Direct the Respondents to fully remunerate the Petitioner for the services rendered by him to "Lyari Expressway Resettlement Project" as a Consultant from joining till date as well as any future services till June, 2021. iii. Declare that the inaction of the Respondent No.3 to remunerate the Petitioner or to pay the Petitioner his lawful pending & outstanding salaries is illegal, unlawful, unconstitutional, mala fide, and without any authority under the law. iv. Any other relief that this Honorable Court deems appropriate and proper in the circumstances of the instant case.” 2. Learned counsel for the petitioner submitted that the outstanding amount, as approved by the Chief Executive, has not yet been paid and requires recalculation with proper reasoning. 3. The learned AAG stated that the amount, after coordination with the petitioner, shall be disbursed within 45 days.

Learned counsel for the petitioner submitted that the outstanding amount, as approved by the Chief Executive, has not yet been paid and requires recalculation with proper reasoning. 3. The learned AAG stated that the amount, after coordination with the petitioner, shall be disbursed within 45 days. The request so made seems to be reasonable and acceded to. 4. Accordingly, the petition stands disposed of in these terms. JUDGE Shahzad Soomro JUDGE
